final_text:
**Analysis Summary:**

I analyzed the dataset `pone.0150501.s002.xls`, which contains Ct values for 175 miRNAs measured in 10 DM1 patients (P_1–P_10) and 10 healthy controls (C_11–C_20). I performed Welch's t-test on the raw Ct values for each miRNA, then applied both Bonferroni and Benjamini-Yekutieli (BY) multiple-testing corrections across all 175 tests.

**Results:**

| Correction | Threshold (α=0.05) | Significant miRNAs |
|---|---|---|
| Uncorrected | p ≤ 0.05 | 63 |
| Bonferroni | p ≤ 0.05/175 = 0.000286 | 0 |
| Benjamini-Yekutieli | BY-adjusted p ≤ 0.05 | 0 |

The smallest raw p-value (hsa-miR-20b, p = 0.000471) exceeds the Bonferroni threshold of 0.000286. The smallest BY-adjusted p-value is 0.2024. With 175 parallel tests, the multiple-testing burden is too high for any miRNA to survive correction at α = 0.05 under either method.

FINAL ANSWER: 0:0
